pattern: Try each of the three possible scores derived from the first and last two elements, then for each fixed score use interval DP with memoization to maximise operations on the subarray [lo, hi].
hints: The target score must equal one of nums[0]+nums[1], nums[-1]+nums[-2], or nums[0]+nums[-1] since the very first operation determines the score.
For a fixed target, define dp(lo, hi) = max operations achievable on the subarray nums[lo..hi]; at each step you can remove from the front, back, or one from each end if the pair sums to target.
Use top-down memoization keyed on (lo, hi) with early termination when hi-lo < 1; take the answer as max over the three candidate scores.

edge_inputs.description: all three operations valid at each step
two operations possible with front-back removal
score from first two differs from last two, only one is achievable longer
greedy fails but DP finds more operations

stress.description: small random arrays where brute-force DP is tractable
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 200))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
